metadata.summary: Chat Completions endpoint guide: request structure, model selection, parameters, streaming, vision, error handling, rate limits, cost tracking via tiktoken.

content.title: OpenAI Chat Completions
content.summary: Complete guide to the OpenAI Chat Completions endpoint (`/v1/chat/completions`): request structure, model selection, parameters (temperature, max_tokens, response_format), streaming, vision (URL and base64), error handling with exponential backoff, rate limit headers, and cost tracking via tiktoken. The core rule: always read `stop_reason` — `"max_tokens"` means silent truncation; never parse JSON from a truncated response.

why.p: Chat Completions is the primary OpenAI inference endpoint. Without proper parameter discipline (temperature for task type, response_format for JSON extraction) and retry logic, pipelines produce inconsistent outputs and fail silently under rate limiting. Vision token cost varies 85–1105 tokens per image depending on detail level — untracked, this blows budgets on multimodal pipelines.

list.item: Building agent pipelines calling OpenAI models (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o1, o3-mini)
Streaming partial outputs to users or downstream pipeline steps in real time
Generating structured JSON via `response_format={"type": "json_object"}`
Multi-image or screenshot analysis inside an automated workflow
Cost-sensitive pipelines where gpt-4o-mini quality is acceptable
When persistent conversation state is needed across sessions — use Assistants API
When guaranteed schema compliance is required — use Structured Outputs (`beta.parse`) not JSON Mode
Tasks requiring more than 128K context — use Claude 200K or Gemini 2M
When Anthropic Claude is available and task quality matters more than vendor diversity

section.name: request-structure-models-and-response-parsing
section.p: Chat Completions request structure, model table with pricing, parameters reference, response parsing, and message roles.

example.code:
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI() # uses OPENAI_API_KEY env var
response = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o", messages=[ {"role": "system", "content": "You are a technical writer specializing in AI."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Write a 200-word introduction about AI for developers."} ], temperature=0.7, max_tokens=4096, response_format={"type": "json_object"}, # force JSON output seed=42 ) print(response.choices[0].message.content)

list.item: gpt-4o: 128K ctx, $2.50/$10.00 per M tokens — general purpose, best quality
gpt-4o-mini: 128K ctx, $0.15/$0.60 per M tokens — cost-effective inner loops
o1: 128K ctx, $15.00/$60.00 per M — complex reasoning
o3-mini: 200K ctx, $1.10/$4.40 per M — latest reasoning model

rule.statement: Use gpt-4o-mini for agent inner loops (cheap, fast); escalate to gpt-4o only for quality gates. Never use gpt-4-turbo for new code — replaced by gpt-4o.
rule.rationale: Cost difference between gpt-4o-mini and gpt-4o is 16x on input tokens. Inner loops typically run thousands of times per pipeline run.

rule.statement: Always check finish_reason — "length" means the output was truncated by max_tokens; never parse truncated JSON blindly, detect and retry with larger max_tokens.
rule.rationale: Truncated JSON silently corrupts downstream pipeline stages; the error appears in the consumer, not at the generation site.

example.code:
response = client.chat.completions.create(model="gpt-4o", messages=[...])
choice = response.choices[0] if choice.finish_reason == "length": raise ValueError("Response truncated — increase max_tokens")
content = choice.message.content usage = response.usage print(f"Tokens: {usage.prompt_tokens} in, {usage.completion_tokens} out")

list.item: system: set behavior, persona, and output format instructions
user: user input or pipeline-injected content
assistant: model responses to include for multi-turn context
tool: tool/function results in function calling workflows

section.name: streaming-and-async-parallel-requests
section.p: Streaming with stream=True for real-time output; AsyncOpenAI with asyncio.gather for parallel pipeline calls.

rule.statement: Use AsyncOpenAI for any pipeline that spawns more than 2 parallel LLM calls; parallel async calls count toward the same TPM budget — throttle with a semaphore for large batches.
rule.rationale: Sequential calls at Tier 1 (500 RPM) can saturate the rate limit; async gather distributes the same calls within the time window, not beyond it.

example.code:
import asyncio from openai import AsyncOpenAI
async_client = AsyncOpenAI()
async def parallel_completions(prompts: list[str]) -> list[str]: async def get_completion(prompt: str) -> str: response = await async_client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o-mini", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}] ) return response.choices[0].message.content
return await asyncio.gather(*[get_completion(p) for p in prompts])
results = asyncio.run(parallel_completions(["Hello", "World", "!"]))

section.name: vision-api
section.p: Image understanding via URL or base64 encoding; detail level controls token cost (85–1105 tokens per image); max 20MB, formats PNG/JPEG/GIF/WebP.

list.item: low: 85 tokens — quick overview, thumbnails, no fine details
high: 85-1105+ tokens — OCR, detailed analysis, full resolution
auto: model decides based on image content

rule.statement: Profile image token cost before shipping multimodal pipelines; high-detail images add up to 1105 tokens each, multiplying costs on batches.
rule.rationale: Vision token cost is proportional to image size and detail level; untracked, it dominates pipeline cost for image-heavy workloads.

list.item: Vision limitations: struggles with rotated text, small text, spatial reasoning, animated GIFs
Max 20 images per message; no video (extract frames manually)
Supported formats: PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP

section.name: error-handling-rate-limits-and-cost-tracking
section.p: Common error types and HTTP codes, exponential backoff with tenacity, rate limit headers for proactive throttling, and token cost tracking with tiktoken.

list.item: invalid_api_key (401): bad OPENAI_API_KEY — check env var
rate_limit_exceeded (429): too many requests or quota — implement backoff
insufficient_quota (429): billing limit reached — add credits or wait for reset
model_not_found (404): invalid model name — check available models
context_length_exceeded (400): input too long — chunk or use larger context model
server_error (500): OpenAI issue — retry with backoff

section.name: retry-with-tenacity
section.reference: OpenAI client wrapper with tenacity retry

rule.statement: Check x-ratelimit-remaining-requests before dispatching large batches; rate limits are per-model, not global — hitting gpt-4o limits does not affect gpt-4o-mini.
rule.rationale: A Tier 1 account has 500 RPM for gpt-4o but higher limits for gpt-4o-mini; checking the header prevents rejected requests on the first call of a large batch.

example.note: tiktoken counts are approximate for multimodal messages — images add 85–1105 tokens depending on detail level, not counted by tiktoken.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A Claude subagent can drive Chat Completions by constructing message arrays, calling the OpenAI SDK, parsing the response, and routing the output to the next pipeline stage. For batch parallelism, use `AsyncOpenAI` with `asyncio.gather`. Always extract `usage` tokens and log them per call to detect cost anomalies. Stop sequences and `n=1` keep outputs predictable in automated pipelines.

list.item: faion-sdd-executor-agent — executes code-generation or review tasks using Chat Completions as the inference backend
nero-sdd-executor-agent — same pattern for NERO platform pipelines

example.title: Minimal agentic call with structured-ish output
example.code: # Minimal agentic call with structured-ish output response = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o-mini", messages=[ {"role": "system", "content": "Return only valid JSON."}, {"role": "user", "content": task_prompt} ], response_format={"type": "json_object"}, temperature=0.2 ) result = json.loads(response.choices[0].message.content)

example.title: Async parallel — N prompts in one pass
example.code:
# Async parallel — N prompts in one pass import asyncio from openai import AsyncOpenAI client = AsyncOpenAI()
async def batch(prompts): tasks = [client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o-mini", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": p}] ) for p in prompts] return await asyncio.gather(*tasks)

list.item: Use `gpt-4o-mini` for agent inner loops (cheap, fast); escalate to `gpt-4o` only for quality gates
Always read `response.usage` and accumulate token counts per pipeline run
Set `temperature=0.0-0.2` for deterministic extraction tasks; `0.7+` only for creative generation
Use `stop=["```"]` or similar when extracting code blocks to prevent runaway outputs
Prefer `AsyncOpenAI` for any pipeline that spawns > 2 parallel LLM calls
Check `x-ratelimit-remaining-requests` header before dispatching large batches
Do not mix JSON Mode with ambiguous system prompts — be explicit: "Return only valid JSON"
Log `model`, `usage`, and `stop_reason` for every call; `max_tokens` stop reason silently truncates output
`stop_reason == "max_tokens"` means output is truncated — never parse truncated JSON blindly; detect and retry with larger `max_tokens`
Token counting with `tiktoken` is approximate for multimodal (images add 85–1105 tokens depending on detail level)
`seed` does not guarantee identical outputs across API versions or after model updates
JSON Mode can return `{}` or minimal JSON when model is confused — always validate schema before passing to next step
Rate limit headers are per-model, not global — a Tier 1 account can hit limits on gpt-4o while gpt-4o-mini is free
Parallel async calls all count toward the same TPM budget — throttle with a semaphore for large batches
Vision + long text in one message inflates context significantly; profile token cost before shipping

template.purpose: OpenAI client wrapper with tenacity retry on RateLimitError and APIError
Base64 image encoding helper for vision requests
